channel.title: OOIR Trends: Clinical Medicine (Integrative & Complementary Medicine)
channel.description: Aktuelle Paper-Trends im Bereich Clinical Medicine (Integrative & Complementary Medicine) von OOIR (mit Titel und Metadaten von Crossref)

item.title: Damage-induced muscle regeneration after exercise in humans: Modulatory effects of ginsenoside Rg1
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 14 Autoren: Andrew Mark Edwards, Peggy Pui Lai Or, Chien-Wen Hou, Chih-Yang Huang, Chen Zheng, Fenghua Sun, Xinmin Liu, Kunanya Masodsai, Chia-Hua Kuo Journal: Journal of Traditional and Complementary Medicine Veröffentlicht: 2026-07-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.jtcme.2026.04.002 ISSN: 2225-4110 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-17

item.title: Role of an Ayurvedic formulation ‘Kalyanaka Ghrita’ in mild cognitive impairment: A single-patient, quasi-randomized, controlled, open-label N-of-1 trial
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 13 Autoren: Swati Sharma, Arun Kaushik, Sankha Shubhra Chakrabarti, Kishor Patwardhan Journal: Journal of Ethnopharmacology Veröffentlicht: 2026-12-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.jep.2026.122089 ISSN: 0378-8741 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-17

item.title: Polyphenols extracted from Lycium barbarum seeds regulate gut microbiota to improve type 2 diabetes mellitus in db/db mice
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 7 Autoren: Juntao Zhang, Rongyi Gong, Yichen Liu, Jianjun Deng, Jinfeng Wang, Haixia Yang Journal: Phytomedicine Veröffentlicht: 2026-09-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.phymed.2026.158507 ISSN: 0944-7113 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-17

item.title: Targeting the Nrf2 Signaling Pathway: A Review of Traditional Chinese Medicine for Alzheimer’s Disease
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 7 Autoren: Ming Liu, Hang Hong, Xingxing Zhang, Jiayu Ren, Chunlan Tang Journal: The American Journal of Chinese Medicine Veröffentlicht: 2026-01-01 Abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder primarily characterized by β-amyloid (Aβ) deposition, Tau protein hyperphosphorylation, and chronic neuroinflammation. Current pharmacological interventions demonstrate limited therapeutic efficacy. The nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) signaling pathway, a pivotal regulator of oxidative stress and neuroinflammation, plays a critical role in AD pathogenesis. Recent studies have revealed that traditional Chinese medicines (TCMs) and their bioactive constituents can modulate the Nrf2 signaling pathway to mitigate oxidative stress, suppress neuroinflammation, enhance Aβ clearance, and reduce Tau protein phosphorylation. By doing so, TCMs exert multi-targeted anti-AD effects. This review systematically summarizes the mechanisms and recent advances concerning the active ingredients of Nrf2 pathway-modulating TCMs, herbal medicines, and TCM formulations for the prevention and treatment of AD. Furthermore, it critically evaluates current research limitations and prospects for future research directions to provide a theoretical foundation for the development of novel anti-AD therapeutics derived from TCMs. DOI: 10.1142/s0192415x26500400 ISSN: 0192-415X Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-17

item.title: Network meta-analysis of different traditional Chinese medicine therapies as adjuvant treatments for Alzheimer's disease
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 7 Autoren: Zongbao Li, Xiuhua Bai, Jiping Li, Wenyi Geng, Xin Xing, Jianqing Liang, Jintian Li, Juan Li Journal: Complementary Therapies in Clinical Practice Veröffentlicht: 2026-08-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.ctcp.2026.102075 ISSN: 1744-3881 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-17
